manufacturer.comment: Banana Pi is the brand operated by Shenzhen SINOVOIP Co., Ltd.

release-date.comment: Public sale opened on AliExpress on 30 Jan 2024 ($165 for 8GB/64GB); originally announced in Nov 2023.

status.comment: Listed on official Banana Pi product catalog and BPI Shop as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: BPI publishes schematic PDFs on GitHub but no PCB design source files under an OSHW license; per RESEARCH.md this does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed by SINOVOIP, headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

soc.comment: Full RK3588 (not RK3588S); enables PCIe 3.0 x4 M.2 and dual 2.5GbE.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Stock max clock for the A76 cluster on RK3588.

process-node.comment: Vendor-stated 8 nm; Rockchip describes RK3588 as Samsung 8LPP-class.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G610 MP4 supports Vulkan 1.1 per BPI specs.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted 6 TOPS @ INT8 across 3 NPU cores; INT4/INT8/INT16 mixed-precision supported.

hw-video-decode.comment: 8K@60 H.265/VP9, 8K@30 H.264, 4K@60 AV1 per vendor specs.

hw-video-encode.comment: Vendor-stated 8K@30 H.265/H.264 encode; AV1 and VP9 not listed for encode.

ram-type.comment: Vendor specs say "64-bit LPDDR4/LPDDR4x"; retail SKUs are typically LPDDR4x.

emmc.comment: Onboard 32/64/128 GB eMMC depending on SKU.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; storage expansion via M.2 NVMe only.

bluetooth.comment: AP6275P provides BT 5.2; vendor wiki occasionally writes "BT5" generically.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB Type-C 3.1 with DP 1.4 alt-mode and OTG; same port also accepts USB-C PD power input.

poe-support.comment: No PoE feature in vendor specs and no documented PoE HAT path on the 40-pin header.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 1× HDMI 2.1; additional displays available via USB-C DP-Alt and MIPI-DSI.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.1 up to 8K@60; USB-C DP 1.4 listed as 8K@30.

displayport.comment: No native DP connector; USB-C DP-Alt mode (DP 1.4) provides DisplayPort output, which RESEARCH.md says counts.

audio-jack.comment: Vendor lists "HP audio out" as an on-board header/speaker connector, not a 3.5 mm jack.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Vendor advertises the 40-pin header as "fully compatible with Raspberry Pi" pinout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Board is 92×62 mm with non-RPi mounting hole positions, so HAT mechanical compliance is not met even though the GPIO pinout matches.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART are all available on the 40-pin header.

idle-power-w.comment: No measured idle figure published by vendor or in major reviews (CNX-Software, Liliputing); per RESEARCH.md prefer null over a TDP guess.

load-power-w.comment: Same as idle: no independently measured full-load figure available; RK3588 boards typically draw 7-10 W under load but this specific board's value is unverified.

cooling-required.comment: Per RESEARCH.md guidance, RK3588 boards under sustained load are typically active-required; BPI ships the board with a heatsink+fan kit.

rtc-battery-header.comment: 2-pin battery connector wired to an HYM8563TS RTC.

form-factor-class.comment: BPI markets it as Pico-ITX-class, but Pico-ITX is 100×72 mm; at 92×62 mm with non-Pi holes it does not match any defined preset and is best classified as custom.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Holes do not match the RPi standard pattern; vendor publishes its own DXF.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published in the wiki, product page, or major reviews.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Boots upstream/Collabora kernel and is supported in Armbian (current 6.18 + vendor 6.1.115); like other RK3588 boards, GPU/NPU and some video acceleration still rely on out-of-tree pieces, so partial rather than full.

official-os-image.comment: Vendor publishes Android 12, Debian 11, and Buildroot images.

distros-with-images.comment: Debian (vendor), Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 (third-party builds endorsed by BPI), and Armbian official build. Buildroot/Kylin omitted as they are not in the defined tag set.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot; mainline U-Boot also boots the board per Collabora work.

long-term-support.comment: BPI does not publish a formal support-window commitment; community track record on BPI boards is mixed, so unclear is the honest tag.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch AliExpress price for 8GB/64GB at January 2024 sale; BPI Shop currently lists $292 for the same SKU (checked 2026-04-26), so retail markup is significant.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Vendor has not published an MSRP for the 32GB/128GB top SKU; resellers list highly variable prices and BPI Shop only exposes the 8/64 price publicly. Null pending an authoritative source.

available-regions.comment: Sold via AliExpress (global), Amazon US, Newegg, ameriDroid (NA), and BPI Shop (China/global) as of 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at multiple major resellers as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Vendor wiki has full spec tables, schematic PDFs, and getting-started guide, but coverage is patchy compared to Raspberry Pi or Radxa docs and several pages link out to forum threads for details.

community-size.comment: Active vendor forum but with modest traffic; broader RK3588 community is large, but BPI-M7-specific community sits in the small bucket per RESEARCH.md thresholds.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum has a dedicated BPI-M7 subforum with regular activity.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vendor offers a heatsink/fan kit and bundles, but no significant ecosystem of cases, displays, or HATs targeted specifically at the BPI-M7. Generic 40-pin HATs work mechanically only on a case-by-case basis.
